title: anyctl catalog index
description: Schema for anyctl-catalog.yaml — the required index file at the root of a directory or git catalog source. It is the intentional-publish marker and identity record for a catalog: it names the catalog, describes it for a marketplace listing, and optionally curates which manifests are members. It is NOT a service manifest (see manifest.schema.json). Every object is closed (additionalProperties: false); a typo'd key is an error, not a silent drop. This schema is an editor aid; the authoritative check is `anyctl catalog validate` (manifest.ParseCatalogIndex), which also enforces the member-filename and name rules JSON Schema cannot fully express.

name.description: Default install name (a single path segment; --name overrides it). Must match ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]*$.

description.description: One-line human-readable summary, shown by `anyctl catalog list`/`info`.

version.description: Optional, informational version string (e.g. "1.0.0"); shown by `anyctl catalog list`/`info`. anyctl does not interpret it.

homepage.description: Optional project/repository URL, shown by `anyctl catalog info`.

items.description: A bare manifest filename (no path separators), ending in .yaml or .yml.

manifests.description: Optional member list. Absent: every top-level *.yaml/*.yml (except this index) is a member. Present: exactly these files, in this order, are installed — curation, ordering, subsets.
